steps.title: Simplify the given equation
steps.content: Start with $$3(x-n)=9y+15n.$$ Expand and collect terms: $$3x-3n=9y+15n$$ $$3x-9y=18n.$$ Divide by $3$: $$x-3y=6n.$$

steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: A two-line system has no solution when the lines are parallel but distinct. That means the second equation should have the same $x$ and $y$ coefficients as $x-3y=6n$, but a different constant.

steps.title: Graph a representative case
steps.content: Since $n$ is any positive constant, choose the easiest positive value, $n=1$. The given equation becomes $x-3y=6$. Choice D becomes $x-3y=3$. Graphing those two lines in Desmos shows parallel, distinct lines, which means no intersection and no solution.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A, $x-3y=6n$, is the exact same line, so it would give infinitely many solutions. Choices B and C use $x+3y$, so they do not have the same slope as the given line. Choice D, $x-3y=3n$, has the same left side but a different constant. Combining it with $x-3y=6n$ would imply $6n=3n$, so $n=0$, contradicting $n>0$. Choice D is correct.
